finding_results.evidence: manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_11.md:19 retains the single kept instance ('The refrigerator clicked — its forty-minute cycle, precise as a metronome'). manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_11.md:184 now reads 'A pipe settled in the wall behind the couch' replacing former 'The refrigerator clicked'. manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_11.md:240 now reads 'The building settled beneath them, a creak traveling through the floorboards' replacing former 'The refrigerator clicked'. manual_rescue/chapters/chapter_11.md:126 revised from 'the refrigerator clicked every forty minutes' to 'the refrigerator running its forty-minute cycle' to eliminate a fourth embedded occurrence of the exact phrase.
finding_results.notes: Three ambient-beat instances reduced to one (line 19). Replacement details maintain domestic grounding: plumbing settling (line 184) and building creak through floorboards (line 240) are appropriate physical sounds for an old Park Slope brownstone walk-up. A fourth embedded occurrence in Dan's recognition list (line 126) was also adjusted to avoid the exact 'refrigerator clicked' phrasing while preserving the forty-minute-cycle detail. No new recurring patterns introduced; each replacement appears once.

summary: Single finding (F02, MEDIUM) addressed via targeted lexical substitution. Refrigerator-click ambient motif reduced from three standalone instances to one (the strongest, at line 19, with its 'precise as a metronome' phrasing). Two replacement ambient details — a water pipe settling in the wall (line 184) and the building settling with a creak through the floorboards (line 240) — preserve domestic grounding and the apartment's physical presence without repeating the motif. One additional embedded occurrence in a subordinate clause (line 126) was adjusted to remove the exact repeated phrase. No structural changes, no collateral churn, no new patterns introduced.
